mechanism: Prime-target word pairs presented in rapid sequence (350-2000ms stimulus onset asynchrony) activate semantic associations in memory, facilitating response time to related targets. Effect modulated by perceiver intentions (stereotype vs. counterstereotype expectancy) and cognitive resource constraints. Reaction times measured as dependent variable, with shorter RTs indicating stronger semantic/stereotypic associations.

Actor updates posterior belief about own prejudice level via observation of past behavior. Nonprejudiced past behavior provides evidence, reducing actor's fear that future ambiguous behavior will be attributed to prejudice. This licensing mechanism operates via self-image reaffirmation rather than impression management alone, allowing expression of attitudes that could appear prejudiced if attribution were unambiguous.

Self-regulation modeled as limited resource (strength) that depletes with use, analogous to muscular fatigue. Initial effortful regulation (emotion suppression, thought suppression, attention control, physical endurance task) consumes regulatory capacity, reducing available resources for subsequent self-regulatory tasks. Performance on second regulation task predicted by remaining capacity after first task depletion.

Longitudinal classification of development trajectories using mixture of latent psychological dimensions (Environmental Mastery and Personal Growth, assessed via validated scales). Growth modeled via latent variable structure connecting personality traits at baseline, identity formation at midpoint, and outcome pattern classification at endpoint. Mediation pathways estimated across 31-year lifespan.

Actor-observer asymmetry in knowledge attribution: actors observe others' behavior and overt utterances, leading to situational underweighting and trait overestimation (external attribution error). Actors simultaneously overweight their own private thoughts and feelings in self-assessment (internal attribution). Asymmetry modeled via differential salience/availability of behavioral vs. internal information across perspectives.
